page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 virtual CPUs (48 physical cores with hyperthreading) running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The hardware configuration also includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a multi-core stress-ng score of 92868.95 and a Passmark CPU score of 74908.92, placing it in the top tier for parallel processing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5008.9 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 121.62 GB/sec. Database operations are a key strength, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 29638.46 and a Redis SET throughput of 88.2 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1589.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1891.98.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2iedn.24xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, reducing the need to scale horizontally solely for RAM capacity. The inclusion of 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local caching without relying entirely on external block storage. This profile is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise-grade relational database management systems, heavy electronic design automation (EDA) workloads, and real-time data analytics pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 3072.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 92868.95 and a Redis SET throughput of 88.2 million operations per second, though single-core metrics remain average. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage capacity make the instance highly efficient for large in-memory databases, enterprise database operations, and intensive data analytics workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 3072 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 3 TB DDR4 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring three terabytes of RAM and local NVMe storage for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
3072 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32 GB of RAM per core
2850 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance in benchmarks
Optimized for large in-memory databases and enterprise workloads
